title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Install all required packages for MF-ACD experiments including causal-learn, gCastle for baselines, and scientific computing libraries. Uses CPU-only environment.

steps.step1: Activate virtual environment: source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install core dependencies: pip install numpy scipy scikit-learn networkx matplotlib seaborn pandas
steps.step3: Install causal discovery libraries: pip install causal-learn gcastle
steps.step4: Install additional utilities: pip install statsmodels tqdm
steps.step5: Verify installation by importing key modules: python -c 'import causallearn; import gcastle'
steps.step6: Record environment versions to versions.txt for reproducibility

title: Generate Synthetic Benchmark Datasets
description: Generate synthetic causal graphs with known ground truth for controlled evaluation. Uses Erdős-Rényi and scale-free (Barabási-Albert) graph models with linear Gaussian SEM.

steps.step1: Create data/synthetic/ directory structure
steps.step2: Generate Erdős-Rényi DAGs: p=20,50,100 nodes; edge_prob=0.1,0.2,0.3; sample_sizes=500,1000,2000
steps.step3: Generate Scale-free DAGs (Barabási-Albert): p=20,50,100 nodes; m=1,2,3; same sample sizes
steps.step4: For each configuration, generate 10 datasets with seeds [42, 123, 456, 789, 1011, 1213, 1415, 1617, 1819, 2021]
steps.step5: Save ground truth adjacency matrices and graph structures
steps.step6: Generate dataset statistics: number of edges, average degree, max in-degree
steps.step7: Create data manifest file listing all datasets with metadata

title: Prepare Real-World Datasets
description: Download and prepare real-world datasets with known causal structures for validation.

steps.step1: Download Sachs protein signaling network (11 nodes, 853 samples) from bnlearn repository
steps.step2: Download Child lung function dataset (20 nodes) from causeffect package
steps.step3: Preprocess: handle missing values, standardize continuous variables
steps.step4: Create train/validation splits (80/20) for hyperparameter tuning
steps.step5: Save processed datasets to data/real_world/ directory
steps.step6: Document data provenance and preprocessing steps

title: Baseline 1: Standard PC Algorithm with Fisher Z
description: Run standard PC algorithm using Fisher Z-test as the baseline for comparison. This represents the full medium-fidelity approach.

steps.step1: Implement wrapper around causallearn's PC algorithm
steps.step2: Configure: independence_test='fisherz', alpha=0.05, stable=False
steps.step3: Run on all synthetic datasets (20, 50, 100 nodes) × (0.1, 0.2 density) × 10 seeds
steps.step4: Record metrics: SHD, F1-score, precision, recall, number of CI tests performed, wall-clock time
steps.step5: Save results to results/baselines/pc_fisherz/
steps.step6: Expected runtime: ~30 seconds per 50-node graph, ~5 minutes per 100-node graph

title: Baseline 2: PC-Stable Algorithm
description: Run PC-Stable (Colombo & Maathuis, 2014) to ensure fair comparison with order-independent variant.

steps.step1: Configure causallearn PC with stable=True option
steps.step2: Use same parameters as Baseline 1: Fisher Z-test, alpha=0.05
steps.step3: Run on same dataset configurations (20, 50, 100 nodes) × 10 seeds
steps.step4: Record identical metrics as Baseline 1
steps.step5: Save results to results/baselines/pc_stable/
steps.step6: Compare PC vs PC-Stable to quantify order-dependence effects

title: Baseline 3: Fast PC (Correlation-Only)
description: Run PC with correlation-based tests only as simple baseline representing pure low-fidelity approach.

steps.step1: Implement correlation-based CI test using Pearson correlation with Fisher Z transformation
steps.step2: Configure: alpha=0.05, use correlation for all conditioning set sizes
steps.step3: Run on all dataset configurations
steps.step4: Record metrics: focus on speed vs accuracy trade-off
steps.step5: Save results to results/baselines/fast_pc/
steps.step6: This serves as the 'cheap but potentially inaccurate' lower bound

title: Baseline 4: HCCD (Hierarchical Causal Discovery)
description: Implement HCCD (Shanmugam et al., 2021) as strong baseline using hierarchical clustering for causal discovery. Reference: arXiv:2107.05001.

steps.step1: Implement HCCD algorithm: (1) hierarchical clustering with normalized min-cut, (2) local PC on clusters, (3) merge with correlation-based linking
steps.step2: Parameters: cluster_threshold=0.5, local_alpha=0.05
steps.step3: Run on 20 and 50 node graphs (HCCD overhead significant for 100 nodes)
steps.step4: Record same metrics as other baselines plus clustering statistics
steps.step5: Save results to results/baselines/hccd/
steps.step6: Document differences: HCCD clusters variables, MF-ACD selects test fidelities

title: Baseline 5: GES (Greedy Equivalence Search)
description: Run GES as a score-based baseline for comparison with constraint-based methods.

steps.step1: Use causallearn's GES implementation with BIC score
steps.step2: Run on all synthetic datasets and real-world datasets
steps.step3: Record metrics: SHD, F1, wall-clock time
steps.step4: Save results to results/baselines/ges/
steps.step5: Compare constraint-based vs score-based approach efficiency

title: Baseline 6: DCILP (Dong et al., 2025)
description: Reference implementation of DCILP: A Distributed Approach for Large-Scale Causal Structure Learning (AAAI 2025). Uses Markov blanket partitioning and ILP merging.

steps.step1: Implement simplified DCILP: (1) estimate Markov blankets, (2) partition variables, (3) learn local graphs, (4) ILP-based merging
steps.step2: Use Fisher Z-test for local discovery to ensure fair comparison
steps.step3: Run on 20, 50 node graphs (DCILP designed for larger graphs)
steps.step4: Record metrics including partitioning and merging statistics
steps.step5: Save results to results/baselines/dcilp/
steps.step6: Note: Correct reference is Dong et al. (2025) at AAAI, not Laborda et al. (2023)

title: Implement MF-ACD Framework
description: Implement the complete Multi-Fidelity Adaptive Causal Discovery framework with all three phases and UGFS mechanism.

steps.step1: Implement fidelity levels: Low (Pearson/partial correlation), Medium (Fisher Z), High (Distance Correlation proxy for KCI)
steps.step2: Phase 1: Low-fidelity skeleton screening with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control (alpha1=0.10)
steps.step3: Phase 2: Medium-fidelity local refinement with Storey's adaptive FDR (alpha2=0.05)
steps.step4: Phase 3: High-fidelity critical resolution with Holm-Bonferroni correction (alpha3=0.01)
steps.step5: Implement UGFS: (a) Edge uncertainty quantification (p-value variance), (b) Structural importance (Markov blanket size), (c) Information gain approximation
steps.step6: Implement adaptive budget reallocation with regret minimization (eta=0.1)
steps.step7: Implement test dependence handling via effective test count estimation
steps.step8: Quantify UGFS overhead: measure time for uncertainty computation, structural importance, IG computation per edge
steps.step9: Validate implementation on small example (10-node graph) with known output

title: MF-ACD Main Evaluation on Synthetic Data
description: Run MF-ACD on all synthetic benchmarks to test primary hypothesis: 30-50% cost reduction with <5% accuracy loss.

steps.step1: Configure MF-ACD with pilot-informed initial allocation: Phase 1: 34%, Phase 2: 20%, Phase 3: 46% (based on pilot_results.json)
steps.step2: Set cost weights: low=1, medium=1.1, high=15 (based on pilot cost ratios)
steps.step3: Run on all synthetic datasets: (20, 50, 100 nodes) × (0.1, 0.2 density) × 10 seeds = 600 runs
steps.step4: For 100-node graphs: limit high-fidelity tests to prevent timeout; use distance correlation as KCI proxy
steps.step5: Record for each run: final SHD, F1-score, precision, recall, number of tests per phase, total computational cost, wall-clock time
steps.step6: Record UGFS overhead: time for uncertainty computations, edge selection
steps.step8: Parallelize across 2 CPU cores for efficiency

title: MF-ACD Evaluation on Real-World Data
description: Validate MF-ACD on real datasets with known ground truth.

steps.step1: Run on Sachs network (11 nodes, 853 samples) with 10 random seeds
steps.step2: Run on Child dataset (20 nodes) with 10 random seeds
steps.step3: Use same configuration as synthetic experiments
steps.step4: Record metrics: SHD to known ground truth, F1, computational cost
steps.step5: Compare against baselines on same datasets
steps.step6: Save results to results/mf_acd/real_world/

title: Ablation 1: Fixed vs Adaptive Budget Allocation
description: Test whether adaptive allocation (UGFS) outperforms fixed allocation strategies.

steps.step1: Variant A: Fixed allocation using pilot-informed 34/20/46 split (no adaptation)
steps.step2: Variant B: Full MF-ACD with adaptive reallocation based on regret minimization
steps.step3: Run both variants on subset: 50 nodes × 0.1,0.2 density × 10 seeds = 40 runs each
steps.step4: Compare: accuracy (F1), cost, statistical significance (paired t-test)
steps.step5: Hypothesis: Adaptive allocation significantly outperforms fixed (p < 0.05)
steps.step6: Save results to results/ablations/fixed_vs_adaptive/

title: Ablation 2: Budget Allocation Sensitivity
description: Test sensitivity to initial budget allocation percentages.

steps.step1: Variant A: Conservative 40/30/30 (more weight on early phases)
steps.step2: Variant B: Aggressive 25/15/60 (more weight on high-fidelity)
steps.step3: Variant C: Balanced 35/20/45 (close to pilot mean)
steps.step4: Run all variants on 50-node graphs × 10 seeds
steps.step5: Analyze: how sensitive is performance to initial allocation?
steps.step6: Identify optimal allocation ranges for different graph densities

title: Ablation 3: UGFS Component Analysis
description: Test contribution of each UGFS component by systematic removal.

steps.step1: Variant A: No uncertainty quantification (random edge selection)
steps.step2: Variant B: No structural importance weighting (uniform edge importance)
steps.step3: Variant C: No information gain approximation (fixed fidelity per phase)
steps.step4: Variant D: Full UGFS (all components)
steps.step5: Run on 50-node graphs × 10 seeds
steps.step6: Measure impact of each component on accuracy and cost

title: Ablation 4: Multiple Testing Correction Comparison
description: Compare Holm-Bonferroni vs Bonferroni vs no correction in Phase 3.

steps.step1: Variant A: Phase 3 with Holm-Bonferroni (default)
steps.step2: Variant B: Phase 3 with standard Bonferroni (more conservative)
steps.step3: Variant C: Phase 3 with no multiple testing correction
steps.step4: Run on 50-node graphs × 10 seeds
steps.step5: Measure: false positive rate, power (true positive rate), overall F1
steps.step6: Verify Holm-Bonferroni maintains FWER while improving power

title: Validation: Information Gain Approximation
description: Empirically validate the IG approximation: IG ≈ 4·p·(1-p)·Power·Disc.

steps.step1: Generate synthetic graphs with known ground truth
steps.step2: For each edge, compute predicted IG using approximation formula
steps.step3: Measure actual IG: entropy reduction in graph posterior after test
steps.step4: Compute correlation between predicted and actual IG
steps.step5: Test under different dependence structures: (a) independent tests, (b) correlated tests (overlapping conditioning sets)
steps.step6: Generate calibration curves: predicted vs actual binned by predicted value
steps.step7: Success criterion: correlation r > 0.6 (positive correlation, not perfect validity)

title: Validation: UGFS Overhead Quantification
description: Measure the computational overhead of UGFS components.

steps.step1: Profile time for: uncertainty quantification (p-value variance), structural importance (Markov blanket estimation), information gain computation, edge selection (argmax)
steps.step2: Measure on graphs of size 20, 50, 100 nodes
steps.step3: Compare UGFS overhead to total Phase 1 cost
steps.step4: Verify overhead is within 10-20% of Phase 1 cost as estimated
steps.step5: Save detailed timing breakdown to results/validation/ugfs_overhead/

title: Failure Mode Validation
description: Validate theoretical failure modes through targeted experiments.

steps.step1: Dense graphs: Test on edge_prob=0.4, 0.5 graphs (50 nodes) to verify savings diminish
steps.step2: Low sample size: Test n=100, d=10 (conditioning set size) to verify power degradation
steps.step3: Near-deterministic relationships: Generate data with R² > 0.95 to test Phase 1/2 reliability
steps.step4: Non-linear dependencies: Test with sine, polynomial relationships using linear tests in Phase 1/2
steps.step5: For each failure mode, measure: accuracy drop, cost increase, vs normal conditions
steps.step6: Verify mitigations are effective when applied

title: Statistical Analysis and Success Criteria Verification
description: Analyze all results to verify success criteria and test hypotheses with appropriate statistical tests.

steps.step1: Aggregate all experimental results from results/ directory
steps.step2: Criterion 1: Verify ≥30% cost reduction vs full high-fidelity PC on graphs with ≥50 nodes
steps.step3: Criterion 2: Verify F1-score within 5% of full high-fidelity PC (calculate confidence intervals)
steps.step4: Criterion 3: Test adaptive vs fixed allocation with paired t-test, verify p < 0.05
steps.step5: Criterion 4: Verify FPR < 10% across all phases (measure actual false positive rate)
steps.step6: Criterion 5: Verify IG approximation correlation r > 0.5 with actual information gain
steps.step7: Calculate effect sizes (Cohen's d) for all comparisons
steps.step8: Generate summary table: method × accuracy × cost × statistical significance
steps.step9: Document if any criteria failed and why

title: Generate Paper Figures and Tables
description: Create all figures and tables for the research paper.

steps.step1: Figure 1: MF-ACD framework overview diagram (conceptual)
steps.step2: Figure 2: Accuracy vs Cost trade-off plot (Pareto frontier of methods)
steps.step3: Figure 3: Cost reduction percentage by graph size and density
steps.step4: Figure 4: F1-score comparison across baselines and MF-ACD
steps.step5: Figure 5: Ablation study results (bar chart with error bars)
steps.step6: Figure 6: Budget allocation sensitivity heatmap
steps.step7: Figure 7: IG approximation validation - predicted vs actual scatter plot with regression line
steps.step_7b: Report correlation coefficient r and 95% CI on figure
steps.step8: Figure 8: UGFS overhead breakdown (stacked bar chart)
steps.step9: Figure 9: Failure mode analysis - performance degradation under adverse conditions
steps.step10: Table 1: Main results - comparison with all 6 baselines
steps.step12: Table 3: Pilot data summary (budget allocation statistics)
steps.step13: Save all figures to figures/ directory (PDF and PNG formats)

title: Final Summary and Conclusion
description: Generate final summary document with conclusions, limitations, and future work.

steps.step1: Summarize findings: Did MF-ACD achieve 30-50% cost reduction?
steps.step2: Document actual cost savings achieved with confidence intervals
steps.step3: Document accuracy retention (F1 difference with CIs)
steps.step4: Discuss which ablation components were most critical
steps.step5: Document limitations encountered
steps.step6: Generate results/summary.json with key metrics
